content.text:
I will start by creating the basic structure of `regex.py` and then add functions as described. Here is the initial file:

```python
# regex.py

import re
import pprint

class RegexEngine:
    def compile(self, pattern):
        return re.compile(re.sub(r'(?<!\\)\.', r'[^\n]', pattern))

    def match(self, pattern, string, flags=0):
        compiled_pattern = self.compile(pattern)
        return bool(compiled_pattern.match(string))

    def search(self, pattern, string, flags=0):
        compiled_pattern = self.compile(pattern)
        return bool(compiled_pattern.search(string))

    def findall(self, pattern, string, flags=0):
        compiled_pattern = self.compile(pattern)
        return compiled_pattern.findall(string)

    def fullmatch(self, pattern, string, flags=0):
        compiled_pattern = self.compile(pattern)
        return bool(compiled_pattern.fullmatch(string))
```

Now, I will write the test cases to verify that these functions work as expected.

```python
# test_regex.py

import pytest
from regex import RegexEngine

@pytest.mark.parametrize("regex, string, expected", [
    ("a", "a", True),
    ("a", "ab", False),
    (".", "\n", False),
    (".", "a", True),
    ("*a*", "aaa", True),
    ("+a+", "aaa", True),
    ("?a?", "", True),
    ("abc|def", "def", True),
    ("abc|def", "ghi", False),
    ("(a|b)", "c", False),
    ("(a|b)", "a", True),
    ("[abc]", "d", False),
    ("[abc]", "a", True),
    ("\d", "1", True),
    ("\d", "a", False),
    "^def$", "deff", False)
])
def test_regex_engine(regex, string, expected):
    engine = RegexEngine()
    assert (engine.match(regex, string) == expected)

# Run the tests
if __name__ == "__main__":
    pytest.main(["-v", "-s"])
```

Now, I will run these tests to ensure everything is working correctly.
